While not directly addressing preoperative weight loss, the strong focus on post-surgical nutritional support implies that preparing patients preoperatively with proper nutritional optimization — potentially including monitored weight reduction — would align with best practices to mitigate post-op deficiencies and improve outcomes. Therefore, given the context and logical inference from established guidelines, a medically supervised preoperative weight loss program likely improves bariatric surgery outcomes by priming patients for better metabolic stability and reduced risk of post-operative complications.
